title: Delete your account
summary: Let people permanently close their own account from a clearly marked danger zone in settings, after they confirm they really mean it. Once the account is gone, all of their notes go with it and they are signed out and returned to the login screen.
motivation: Expose the backend account-deletion endpoint behind a deliberate settings danger-zone flow.

included: DELETE /api/settings/delete-account remains the backend destructive account deletion contract
delete-account business rules move from routers/settings.py into the settings service module
SettingsView gains a danger-zone confirmation flow for local and OIDC users
successful deletion clears the frontend auth session and returns the user to the login gate
excluded: soft delete, undo, account recovery, or delayed deletion
bulk data export prompts beyond the existing export section
server-side token blacklist or session revocation
changing backend semantics where OIDC users without local passwords use a non-empty confirmation string

design_facts.stack: Mixed Vue 3/TypeScript/Vite frontend plus FastAPI/Python 3.13/SQLAlchemy async/PostgreSQL backend; DTCG design tokens; no router or Pinia.

token_manifest.system: W3C DTCG JSON built with Style Dictionary into CSS custom properties.
token_manifest.consumption_rule: New component CSS consumes semantic custom properties only; primitives are not used in new code.

work_items.title: Permanently close your account
work_items.summary: Move the account-deletion rules into a single settings service that checks the person's password (or the confirmation step for accounts that sign in through an outside provider) before erasing the account and everything it owns.

exposes.function: async delete_account(db: AsyncSession, current_user: User, request: AccountDeleteRequest) -> MessageResponse

behavior: users with password_hash must provide a password that verifies
OIDC users without password_hash keep the existing non-empty confirmation behavior enforced by AccountDeleteRequest
deleting the User cascades notes through the ORM relationship
routers/settings.py remains dependency wiring plus one service call
the response message keeps the deleted username and success wording

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/test_settings.py::TestDeleteAccount tests/integration/test_settings.py::TestDeleteAccountOIDC tests/unit/test_settings_schemas.py::TestAccountDeleteRequest -v
assertions: Local account deletion succeeds only with the correct password and cascades notes.
Wrong password and unauthenticated deletion still fail with the existing statuses.
OIDC account deletion without a local password still accepts the confirmation path.

work_items.title: Confirm and delete your account
work_items.summary: Give the settings page a danger zone where someone confirms their password or types a confirmation, deletes their account, and is signed out and sent back to the login screen, while any error keeps them signed in.

component_map.notes: Use native password/input confirmation and explicit destructive button; no modal required unless existing view structure needs it.

consumes.auth: useAuth().logout clears the local pm_auth session even if the server-side logout call fails after deletion

behavior: local users enter their current password plus a visible typed confirmation before the delete button enables
OIDC users without a local password use the existing confirmation text path and are not asked to manage an IdP password in ParchMark
submit sends { password } to the backend exactly as entered for the selected account type
success calls the auth logout path so App.vue returns to LoginView
API errors render inline and do not clear the session

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/settings.test.ts src/features/settings/useSettings.test.ts src/features/settings/SettingsView.test.ts src/App.test.ts
assertions: The danger-zone form stays disabled until the user supplies the required password/confirmation inputs.
Successful deletion calls DELETE /api/settings/delete-account, clears auth through logout, and returns the app to the login gate.
Backend errors keep the session and show an inline error without deleting local state.
